task.title: Use Environments and Variables
task.shortdesc: Create Postman environments to manage variables such as base URLs and authentication tokens, and switch between development, staging, and production configurations without editing individual requests.

context.p: An environment in Postman is a set of key-value pairs called variables. Variables let you write requests once and run them against different configurations by changing the active environment. For example, the variable {{baseUrl}} can point to http://localhost:3000 in the development environment and to https://api.example.com in the production environment.

step.cmd: Select the + icon to create a new environment.
Type a name for the environment, such as Development, Staging, or Production.
In the variable table, enter variable names and their initial and current values.
To activate the environment, open the environment selector in the upper-right corner of Postman and select the environment name from the dropdown.
step.stepresult: The environment name appears in the selector and Postman resolves all {{variableName}} references in requests using the current values from the active environment.

step.cmd: In your requests, replace hardcoded values with variable references using double curly braces.

r.p: Postman resolves variable references in request URLs, headers, and body content using the values from the active environment. You can switch environments at any time using the environment selector without modifying individual requests.
